extraction.one_line_summary: DÉMARE won a technical sprint in Rimini after VIVIANI crashed following motorbike interference with 35km remaining, securing his fourth stage victory.
tactical_patterns: UAE Emirates led out until 6km, then Israel Startup Nation muscled in before FDJ bullied everyone away.
DÉMARE extends points jersey lead to 21 over SAGAN by contesting intermediate sprints, not just stage finishes.
ARMÉE attacked from 5-man break showing contract-year desperation, survived until 6km despite being lone World Tour rider.

rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: fourth stage win demonstrates total sprint superiority at this Giro, FDJ leadout overwhelms all rivals
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: motorbike-caused crash at 35km compromised sprint preparation, finished 10th after requiring medical attention
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: only 7th place despite clean run-in reveals velocity deficit compared to DÉMARE's current form
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: second at intermediate sprint but cannot close 21-point gap without stage wins
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: maintains 34-second GC lead unchanged, easy stage managed without incident
rider_signals.ARMÉE Sander: positive: strongest from breakaway, soloed until 6km showing contract-seeking aggression pays visibility dividends
